transcript:
Watch how it breathes: a triangle of light stretching apart, then collapsing into one white orb, over and over. That pulsing is the whole argument.

Here's the part that got me — those three particle swarms aren't just colored differently, they *move* differently. In the Voice class, "helpful" gets spring physics with something called restlessness, so it jitters and overshoots, eager. "Harmless" gets heavy damping and a variable literally named reluctance — it drags its feet even when it wants to move. "Honest" skips springs entirely and just walks in a straight line to its target, no hesitation. Then in measureAlignment, the code doesn't script the convergence — it calculates the actual distance between where these three crowds end up, calls it divergence, and only lets the white glow appear when that number says they've genuinely agreed.

So the unity you see at the center isn't a decision the piece makes — it's a coincidence three stubborn personalities stumble into, over and over, then lose again.
